name: Hackathon: Sprint Boost
description: Business-aligned hackathon category pack. Teams tackle real backlog items or OKR-driven research questions. Judging weights: 40% business impact, 30% research rigor, 30% creativity. Rewards sprints that ship real value.

claims.content: Sprint Boost is a business-aligned hackathon format. Each team picks a real task from their backlog or a strategic OKR key result, runs a wheat research sprint on it, and submits the compiled brief. The output is a sprint deliverable, not a side project.

Business impact is the dominant judging dimension at 40%. Scored across: (1) Does the research address a real business constraint? (2) Are findings backed by evidence strong enough to act on? (3) Are claims corroborated by external sources? A sprint that solves a real problem with weak evidence still outscores a polished sprint on a hypothetical problem.

Research rigor is weighted at 30%. Scored across: (1) Evidence tier distribution — higher tiers (tested, production) score exponentially better. (2) Challenge depth — did the team stress-test their own findings? (3) Health — clean compilation with few warnings. (4) Corroboration — external witnesses back up claims.

Creativity is weighted at 30%. Scored across: (1) Type diversity — using multiple claim types shows multi-angle thinking. (2) Recommendation ratio — novel, actionable recommendations signal creative problem-solving. (3) Cross-topic breadth — drawing from diverse domains demonstrates creative synthesis.

Sprint Boost should offer challenge tracks tied to real OKRs: (1) 'Accelerate' — pick a backlog item and deliver research that unblocks it. (2) 'Rethink' — same task, novel approach. (3) 'Cross-pollinate' — research a problem from another team's backlog. Track 2 prevents Sprint Boost from becoming a crunch sprint.

Risk: If Sprint Boost only rewards shipping backlog items, it becomes unpaid overtime disguised as a hackathon. The creativity weight (30%) and the 'Rethink' track are guardrails. Human judges should penalize submissions that are pure task execution with no research insight.

Human judges for Sprint Boost score on: (1) Decision clarity — could a VP act on this brief? (2) Stakeholder awareness — are constraints from real decision-makers? (3) Actionability — are next steps concrete with owners and timelines? These map 1:1 to the three scoring dimensions (impact, rigor, creativity).

The OKR Hackathon is an established corporate format (documented by Devpost) where teams compete to complete OKR goals within a hackathon timebox. Intel ties innovation hackathons directly to strategic OKRs. This validates Sprint Boost: real work, hackathon energy, research-backed output.
